chapter_title.en: HADEETH ABOUT THE PEOPLE ON THE DAY OF JUDGEMENT

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 26)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 26)
text: لَقِيَنِي أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ (عليه السلام) فِي طَرِيقِ الْمَدِينَةِ فَقَالَ مَنْ ذَا أَ حَارِثٌ قُلْتُ نَعَمْ قَالَ أَمَا لَأَحْمِلَنَّ ذُنُوبَ سُفَهَائِكُمْ عَلَى عُلَمَائِكُمْ ثُمَّ مَضَى فَأَتَيْتُهُ فَاسْتَأْذَنْتُ عَلَيْهِ فَدَخَلْتُ فَقُلْتُ لَقِيتَنِي فَقُلْتَ لَأَحْمِلَنَّ ذُنُوبَ سُفَهَائِكُمْ عَلَى عُلَمَائِكُمْ فَدَخَلَنِي مِنْ ذَلِكَ أَمْرٌ عَظِيمٌ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ مَا يَمْنَعُكُمْ إِذَا بَلَغَكُمْ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ مِنْكُمْ مَا تَكْرَهُونَ وَ مَا يَدْخُلُ عَلَيْنَا بِهِ الْأَذَى أَنْ تَأْتُوهُ فَتُؤَنِّبُوهُ وَ تَعْذِلُوهُ وَ تَقُولُوا لَهُ قَوْلًا بَلِيغاً فَقُلْتُ [لَهُ] جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ إِذاً لَا يُطِيعُونَا وَ لَا يَقْبَلُونَ مِنَّا فَقَالَ اهْجُرُوهُمْ وَ اجْتَنِبُوا مَجَالِسَهُمْ.

en.hubeali: H 14617 – Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Khataab Bin Muhammad, from Al-Haris Bin Al-Mugheira, who has narrated the following:

Abu Abdullahasws met me in a road of Al-Medina, so heasws said: ‘Who is that? Is it Haaris?’ I said, ‘Yes’. Heasws said: ‘But Iasws shall dump the sins of your foolish ones upon your knowledgeable ones’. Then heasws passed by. So I came up to himasws and sought permission to see himasws. I said, ‘Youasws met me and youasws said, ‘Iasws shall dump the sins of your foolish ones upon your knowledgeable ones’, and that was a great matter upon me’. So heasws said: ‘Yes. What prevented you when it reached you from the man among you what you dislike, as it enters hurtful feelings to usasws, (you should) go to him and reproach him, and correct it (his wrong doings), and speak to him in eloquent words?’ I said to himasws, ‘May I be sacrificed for youasws, if he does not obey us and does not accept from us?’ So heasws said: ‘Flee from him, and avoid their gatherings’ (stop accompanying him).
en.sarwar: 169. Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Khataab Bin Muhammad, from Al-Haris Bin Al-Mugheira, who has narrated the following: Abu Abdullah (a.s.) met me in a road of Al-Medina, so he (a.s.) said: ‘Who is that? Is it Haaris?’ I said, ‘Yes’. He (a.s.) said: ‘But I (a.s.) shall dump the sins of your foolish ones upon your knowledgeable ones’. Then he (a.s.) passed by. So I came up to him (a.s.) and sought permission to see him (a.s.). I said, ‘You (a.s.) met me and you (a.s.) said, ‘I (a.s.) shall dump the sins of your foolish ones upon your knowledgeable ones’, and that was a great matter upon me’. So he (a.s.) said: ‘Yes. What prevented you when it reached you from the man among you what you dislike, as it enters hurtful feelings to us (a.s.), (you should) go to him and reproach him, and correct it (his wrong doings), and speak to him in eloquent words?’ I said to him (a.s.), ‘May I be sacrificed for you (a.s.), if he does not obey us and does not accept from us?’ So he (a.s.) said: ‘Flee from him, and avoid their gatherings’ (stop accompanying him).
